description: Reply to a COMMAND message. Reuses the COMMAND body layout — the command_id echoes the original request's ID so the sender can correlate, and the command_name field carries an error description when the command failed. The command payload in the reply typically contains the result or diagnostic output.

fields.name: command_id
fields.description: Echoed command ID from the original COMMAND message. The sender uses this to match the reply to its outstanding request.

fields.name: command_name
fields.description: On success: echoes the original command name. On error: carries an error name or description string. Null-padded to 128 bytes.

fields.name: encoding
fields.description: IANA MIBenum code for the character encoding of the `command` payload (same as COMMAND).

fields.name: length
fields.description: Byte length of the trailing `command` field.

fields.name: command
fields.description: Reply payload — result data or error diagnostic, exactly `length` bytes. Typically XML. Interpreted per `encoding`.
